title: oleaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank oleaceae is a family of flowering plants, typically characterized by their fragrant flowers and often woody stems. Members of the oleaceae family, commonly known as the olive family, include various species of trees and shrubs. This family is known for its diverse range of species, including the iconic olive tree (Olea europaea) and other ornamental plants like forsythia and lilac. The oleaceae family is found in temperate and subtropical regions around the world, often thriving in well-drained soil and full sun.
